body:
## Summary

- Allow tracked session requesters to use `/terminal_watch` and `/watch` for their own tasks while preserving chat-owner access to all sessions.
- Register `/watch` as an alias, update argument parsing, and refresh Telegram `/help` text, including the new localized help-message keys from `main`.
- Add Issue 1778 case-study data, screenshots, local logs, and a patch changeset.

## Reproduction

Before this change, a non-owner user who started a task hit `❌ /terminal_watch is only available to the chat owner.` The focused regression coverage now verifies requester access, chat-owner fallback access, `/watch` parsing/registration, and private-repository DM failure guidance.

## Verification

- `node tests/test-issue-467-terminal-watch.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-1720-terminal-watch-no-log.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-1686-log-command.mjs`
- `node tests/test-issue-1700-isolation-parsing.mjs`
- `node tests/test-telegram-ui-i18n.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm ci`
- `npm run format:check`
- `npm run lint`
- `npm test` (all 202 selected test files passed)
- `npm run check:duplication` (passes; existing duplicate-code findings are preserved in the case-study log)

Note: `npm ci` completed locally with the existing Node engine warning because this runner has Node 20.20.2 while the package declares `>=24`.

## Evidence

![Terminal watch denied](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1778-4d0ea1ed0c9f/docs/case-studies/issue-1778/assets/terminal-watch-denied.png?raw=true)

![Task requester detected](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/issue-1778-4d0ea1ed0c9f/docs/case-studies/issue-1778/assets/task-owner-detected.png?raw=true)


Fixes #1778
title: Allow terminal watch for task requesters
